search_queries_used: site:entnet.org dysphonia guideline laryngeal lesion follow-up outcomes
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic benign or malignant lesion classification
automatic laryngoscopy, biopsy, voice therapy, medicine, or surgery
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The workflow title does not identify pathology or prior procedure.
No investigation or treatment is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with a clinician-established vocal-cord lesion under follow-up; pathology, age, procedure, airway, and cancer risk require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, urgent, audiology, vestibular, voice, or ENT assessment as qualified by the source.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE ENT, audiology, vestibular, cancer-pathway, prescribing, referral, and emergency adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact dysphonia section supports interval voice quality, duration, vocal demand, tobacco and reflux context, impact, associated symptoms and concerns without characterising the lesion.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports clinician-performed ENT and neck assessment, reviewed specialist reports, and concern documentation without ordering laryngoscopy or asserting progression.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact outcomes section supports clinician-entered discussion, follow-up, safety net, and questions without treatment advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

unresolved_source_gaps: Lesion type, pathology, procedure, laryngoscopy findings, diagnosis, treatment, and surveillance interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
